mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing immediate S&P 500 sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear days with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production indicate supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints that compress corporate profit margins, depressing equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods when INDPRO falls, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI decline below 0.3% signals disinflationary pressure that reduces near-term rate hike expectations, producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll gains, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt accumulation, triggering mean-reversion sell-offs in equities.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in industrial production signal improving factory utilization and output growth, driving positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with rising INDPRO, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recovery regimes.
